briefdescription.para: The mask used to extract the 11-bit Base Identifier from a CAN frame.
The mask used to extract the 29-bit Extended Identifier from a CAN frame.
The maximum number of bytes in the payload of a CAN format frame.
The maximum number of bytes in the payload of a can_msg struct.

detaileddescription.para: If this flag is set, the CAN Extended Format (with a 29-bit identifier) is used, otherwise the CAN Base Format (with an 11-bit identifier) is used.

briefdescription.para: The Remote Transmission Request (RTR) flag (unavailable in CAN FD format frames).

detaileddescription.para: If this flag is set, the frame has no payload.

briefdescription.para: The flags of a CAN or CAN FD format frame.
Simple calculation assuming no bit stuffing.
Exact calculation based of frame content and CRC.
The method used to compute te size (in bits) of a CAN frame.
Computes the size (in bits) of the specified CAN format frame on the CAN bus.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to a CAN frame format frame.
the method used to compute the size (one of CAN_MSG_BITS_MODE_NO_STUFF, CAN_MSG_BITS_MODE_WORST or CAN_MSG_BITS_MODE_EXACT).

simplesect.para: the number of bits on success, or -1 on error. In the latter case, the error code can be obtained with get_errc().

memberdef.argsstring: (uint_least16_t crc, const void *ptr, ptrdiff_t off, size_t bits)
memberdef.name: can_crc

briefdescription.para: Computes a bitwise CRC-15-CAN checksum, based on the 0x4599 generator polynomial.

detaileddescription.para: The implementation uses a table with precomputed values for efficiency.

parameterdescription.para: a pointer to the bits to be hashed.
the offset (in bits) with respect to ptr of the first bit to be hashed.
the number of bits to hash.

briefdescription.para: This header file is part of the CAN library; it contains the CAN frame declarations.

detaileddescription.para:
